Method–provenance block
• Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions — f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), dual descriptor (f⁺ – f⁻); identification of the most electrophilic/nucleophilic atomic sites  
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting)  
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied furan geometry `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz`; single-point N, N + 1, and N – 1 charge-constrained calculations (no re-optimization)  
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model)  
• Charge / multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (q = 0, M = 1); cation q = +1, M = 2; anion q = –1, M = 2  
• Software: PySCF 2.x backend via chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill  
• Convergence & integrity: integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true; SCFs converged for all three charge states (see live log).
